task.title: Deki character card scenario edits normalize to the character data patch contract
task.source: GitHub issue #751: Deki character card edit rejects scenario field draft.

coreClaim: Deki character edit actions that propose direct supported card fields, including scenario, are accepted and canonicalized to patch.data before storage application.
rootCause: Deki's prompt guidance and action validator both required the character storage wrapper shape for edits, but Deki could draft a direct card-field patch such as patch.scenario. The Rust response normalizer rejected that supported card-field intent as an unsupported character draft field before the user could apply it.

inScope: Deki Rust response action parsing and normalization
Prompt guidance for character edit_record card fields
Focused regression coverage for direct scenario edit patches
outOfScope: New Tauri commands or HTTP dispatch routes

claimBoundary.coreClaim: Deki character edit actions that propose direct supported card fields are normalized to the existing patch.data storage shape before validation/application.
entrypoints: normalizeDekiEntryAction loading or receiving Deki action state
currentPathsOrFormats: Character edit_record action JSON with patch.scenario from issue #751
Canonical character storage update action shape: patch.data.scenario
Existing character storage update deep-merge path owned by src-tauri/src/commands/storage/characters.rs
legacyPathsOrFormats: Existing canonical patch.data character edit actions remain accepted unchanged
Character create_record actions still require draft.data and are not normalized from top-level card fields
Invented fields remain rejected by the character card data validator
outOfScopeClaims: This PR does not prove live LLMs will always emit the canonical shape; prompt guidance reduces that risk and the normalizer catches the reported direct field shape.
This PR does not change storage merge semantics; it relies on the existing character data deep-merge behavior.

contractLaneGate.brokenContract: The Deki action producer could emit a direct supported card field for character edits, while the Rust and TypeScript consumers only accepted the nested storage wrapper shape.
contractLaneGate.producer: src-tauri/src/commands/storage/deki.rs build_system_prompt and LLM-produced hidden action JSON
contractLaneGate.impliedContract: Supported character card edit fields should be reviewable/applyable when represented either as the canonical patch.data shape or as a direct card-field edit intent that can be losslessly canonicalized.
contractLaneGate.actualEnforcement: The Rust response normalizer and TypeScript action normalizer wrap direct supported character edit fields into data before validation; Deki prompt guidance now asks for patch.data.<field> for character edits.
contractLaneGate.primaryOwnerDetail: src-tauri owns native Deki response action parsing; src/engine mirrors the action contract for frontend state normalization.
contractLaneGate.wrongLaneFixToAvoid: Do not hide this in the React UI or storage update caller; the action contract must be normalized before invalid action state reaches apply.
regressionProof: src-tauri/src/commands/storage/deki.rs Rust test normalizes patch.scenario into patch.data.scenario
src/shared/api/deki-api.spec.ts TypeScript test normalizes patch.scenario into patch.data.scenario
pnpm check validates the full local PR gate after rebase

positiveRows.claim: Rust Deki response parsing accepts the issue #751 direct scenario edit shape and canonicalizes it to patch.data.scenario.
positiveRows.evidence: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml deki_response_normalizes_character_edit_card_fields_into_data_patch passed after rebase.

positiveRows.claim: TypeScript Deki action normalization preserves the same direct scenario edit as an applyable edit_record action.
positiveRows.evidence: D:\dev\Marinara-Engine\node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run src/shared/api/deki-api.spec.ts passed with 22 tests after rebase.

positiveRows.claim: The branch preserves architecture, storage contracts, frontend compile, Rust compile, docs checks, and workflow checks.
positiveRows.evidence: pnpm check exited 0 after rebasing onto origin/main.

contradictionRows.claim: Character create actions should not silently accept top-level card fields because new character cards require the full draft.data shape.
contradictionRows.evidence: normalizeDekiCharacterPayload returns the original payload when requireCompleteCard is true; existing incomplete character create validation remains in the Rust tests.

contradictionRows.claim: Invented character fields should not be accepted as card edits.
contradictionRows.evidence: Only payloads containing supported character data keys are wrapped; wrapped payloads still run through validate_deki_character_data and its allowed-field list.

contradictionRows.claim: The fix should not add a UI-only guard or fake success path.
contradictionRows.evidence: The diff changes action normalization and prompt guidance only; no React apply handling or storage fake-success branch changed.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Existing patch.data character edit actions remain accepted.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: normalize_deki_character_action_payload and normalizeDekiCharacterPayload return payload unchanged when data is already present.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Existing character storage deep-merge behavior remains the owner of partial data updates.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: No changes were made to src-tauri/src/commands/storage/characters.rs; existing update_character_deep_merges_nested_data_objects test remains in that module.

untestedRows: No live Deki LLM provider call was run; the reported failure shape is covered at the parser/normalizer boundary.

ownedFacts.description: Character storage updates use a deep merge for nested data objects before patching the record.
ownedFacts.evidence: src-tauri/src/commands/storage/characters.rs update_character_inner calls merge_partial_character_data and has update_character_deep_merges_nested_data_objects coverage.

ownedFacts.description: Deki character actions are normalized in both Rust command output and the TypeScript action mirror.
ownedFacts.evidence: src-tauri/src/commands/storage/deki.rs normalize_deki_response_action and src/engine/deki/deki-entry.ts normalizeDekiEntryAction are both changed and tested.

manualBlockers.reason: The core failure was the action parser/normalizer contract. Focused Rust and TS tests cover the rejected JSON shape, and full pnpm check passed.

verification.baselineEvidence: pnpm check exited 0 after rebase onto origin/main; warning-only unused/dead-code reports remain non-blocking.

notes: PR remains draft until proof health, CI, and Bunny gates are clean.

userActionCopy.source: Existing De-Koi character record in the app data characters collection
userActionCopy.destination: Same character record through the existing storage_update character patch path; no manual filesystem copy is needed
userActionCopy.action: No user copy or backup action is required for this code change; character data deep-merge storage behavior is unchanged
detectedLayouts: current character card storage shape: patch.data.<field>
